BaseField.Name: Label60
BaseField.Text: Visit the Forest Practices Forms and Applications page for more information on forest practices applications and forms.

BaseField.Name: Label241
BaseField.Text: Instructions: To upload documentation, select the document type and then select the upload button below. View documentation by selecting the View Documentation button.

BaseField.Name: Label191
BaseField.Text: Select the Landowner type you want to add.*

BaseField.Name: Landowner Business Or Individual
BaseField.AccessibilityLabel: Landowner Business Or Individual Dropdown Required

BaseField.Name: Label192
BaseField.Text: Instructions: Search for a contact below to add a Landowner to your application.

BaseField.Name: Landowner Business Search Business ID Number
Landowner Business Search Postal Zip Code
Label198
BaseField.Text: Instructions: Search for a contact below to add a business signer to your application.

BaseField.Name: Label228
BaseField.Text: Instructions: Search for a contact below to add a Landowner to your application.

BaseField.Name: DG Landowners
BaseField.DataFilter: [Contact Information Relation].[Related Record ID] = '{{Form ID}}' AND [Contact Information Relation].[Related Record ID] <> '' AND [Contact Information Relation].[Landowner] = 'True' AND [Contact Information Relation].[Status] <> 'Disabled' AND [Contact Information Relation].[Business Signer] = 'False'

BaseField.Name: DG Landowner Businesses
BaseField.DataFilter: [Contact Information Relation].[Related Record ID] = '{{Form ID}}' AND [Contact Information Relation].[Landowner] = 'True' AND [Contact Information Relation].[Related Record ID] <> '' AND [Contact Information Relation].[Status] <> 'Disabled'

BaseField.Name: DG Landowners Signers
BaseField.DataFilter: [Contact Information Relation].[Related Record ID] = '{{Form ID}}' AND [Contact Information Relation].[Related Record ID] <> '' AND [Contact Information Relation].[Landowner] = 'True' AND [Contact Information Relation].[Status] <> 'Disabled' AND [Contact Information Relation].[Business Signer] = 'True'

BaseField.Name: First Name Contact Person
BaseField.AccessibilityLabel: First Name Contact Person field Required

BaseField.Name: Last Name Contact Person
BaseField.AccessibilityLabel: Last Name Contact Person field Required

BaseField.Name: Label94
BaseField.Text: 2. Are you a small forest landowner per RCW 76.09.450?*

BaseField.Name: Label96
BaseField.Text: Are you a small forest landowner per RCW 76.09.450? See instructions for details.

BaseField.Name: btn_HelpSmallForestLandowner
BaseField.AccessibilityLabel: Quick Help: Are you small forest landowner

BaseField.Name: Harvest Area Contiguous
BaseField.Text: My entire proposed harvest area is on a single contiguous ownership consisting of one or more parcels.

BaseField.Name: DNR Technical Assistance
BaseField.Text: I received technical assistance from a DNR small forest landowner Stewardship and Technical Assistance Forester

BaseField.Name: Label104
BaseField.Text: 3. What is the legal description of the areas included in this assessment?*

BaseField.Name: btn_HelpLegalDescription
BaseField.AccessibilityLabel: Quick Help: What is the legal description of the areas included in this assessment?

BaseField.Name: DG Legal Description
BaseField.DataFilter: [Related Record ID] = '{{Form ID}}' AND [Related Record ID] <> '' AND [Status] <> 'Disabled'

BaseField.Name: Label106
BaseField.Text: 4. Are there potential unstable slopes or landforms in or around the area of your forest practices activity?*

BaseField.Name: Label108
BaseField.Text: Are there potential unstable slopes or landforms in or around the area of your forest practices activity?*

BaseField.Name: btn_HelpUnstableSlopesLandforms
BaseField.AccessibilityLabel: Quick Help: Are there potential unstable slopes or landforms in or around the area of your forest practices activity?

BaseField.Name: Label107
BaseField.Text: If yes, complete Appendix D. Slope Stability Informational Form and upload any applicable geotechnical letter, memo, or report, Watershed Analysis prescriptions, and/or SEPA Environmental Checklist.

BaseField.Name: DG Appendix D
BaseField.DataFilter: [Related Record ID] <> '' AND [Related Record ID] = '{{Form ID}}' AND [Status] <> 'Disabled'

BaseField.Name: DG Appendix D View
BaseField.DataFilter: [Related Record ID] <> '' AND [Related Record ID] = '{{Form ID}}' AND [Status] <> 'Disabled'

BaseField.Name: Label111
BaseField.Text: 5. Is this Forest Practices Application/Notification (answer all of the following questions)*

BaseField.Name: Label112
BaseField.Text: a. Within 500 feet of a public park?*

BaseField.Name: btn_HelpWithin500FetPublicPark
BaseField.AccessibilityLabel: Quick Help: Within 500 Feet Public Park

BaseField.Name: Label116
BaseField.Text: b. Within 50 miles of saltwater AND you own more than 500 acres of forestland in Washington state?*

BaseField.Name: Within 50 Miles Saltwater Own More Than 500 Acres
BaseField.AccessibilityLabel: Within 50 Miles Saltwater Own More Than 500 Acres

BaseField.Name: btn_HelpWithin50milesSaltWater
BaseField.AccessibilityLabel: Quick Help: Within 50 miles of saltwater

BaseField.Name: Label118
BaseField.Text: Include Appendix J. Marbled Murrelet Form or attach/reference applicable HCP prescriptions.

BaseField.Name: DG Appendix J
BaseField.DataFilter: [Related Record ID] <> '' AND [Related Record ID] = '{{Form ID}}' AND [Status] <> 'Disabled'

BaseField.Name: DG Appendix J View
BaseField.DataFilter: [Related Record ID] <> '' AND [Related Record ID] = '{{Form ID}}' AND [Status] <> 'Disabled'

BaseField.Name: Label120
BaseField.Text: c. Including areas of critical wildlife habitat?*

BaseField.Name: btn_HelpWildlife
BaseField.AccessibilityLabel: Quick Help: Including areas of critical wildlife habitat

BaseField.Name: Label122
BaseField.Text: If yes, document types of habitat and their locations on an activity map separate from the one(s) used for the rest of your assessment

BaseField.Name: Label124
BaseField.Text: d. In or directly adjacent to a potential Channel Migration Zone (CMZ)?*

BaseField.Name: btn_HelpAdjacentMigration
BaseField.AccessibilityLabel: Quick Help: In or directly adjacent to a potential Channel Migration Zone (CMZ)?

BaseField.Name: Label126
BaseField.Text: If yes, upload Appendix E. CMZ Assessment Form or upload/reference applicable HCP and/or Watershed Analysis Prescriptions

BaseField.Name: Label128
BaseField.Text: 6.Stream Segment, Stream Associated Wetland, Lake, or Pond Inventory.

BaseField.Name: Label189
BaseField.Text: Does this Forest Practices include streams, Stream associated Wetlands, Lakes, and/or Ponds?

BaseField.Name: Label245
BaseField.Text: List each one separately and show it, along with their identifier on your activity map.

BaseField.Name: Label132
BaseField.Text: Are you operating within 200 feet of a wetland that is not associated with Type S or F water?

BaseField.Name: Label247
BaseField.Text: Wetlands associated with Type S or F Water should be listed in Question 6. Show the boundaries of each wetland, along with its identifier, and Wetland Management Zone (WMZ) on the Activity Map.

BaseField.Name: Label135
BaseField.Text: 8. Sensitive Site Inventory for Type Np Waters.

BaseField.Name: Label136
BaseField.Text: Does your forest practices include sensitive sites near or adjacent to Type Np water?

BaseField.Name: btn_HelpInventoryNpWaters
BaseField.AccessibilityLabel: Quick Help: Sensitive Site Inventory for Type Np Waters

BaseField.Name: Label250
BaseField.Text: List each one separately and show it, along with their identifier on your activity map.

BaseField.Name: Label139
BaseField.Text: Are private or forest roads being used for this forest practices?

BaseField.Name: Label253
BaseField.Text: Show roads, water crossings, spoil areas, and rock pits, along with their unique identifier on your Activity Map.

BaseField.Name: Label141
BaseField.Text: 10. How are the following currently marked on the ground?

BaseField.Name: btn_HelpMarkedOnTheGround
BaseField.AccessibilityLabel: Quick Help: Currently Marked On The Ground

BaseField.Name: Label146
BaseField.Text: 11. Have you reviewed this forest practices activity area to determine whether it may involve historic sites and/or Native American cultural resources?*

BaseField.Name: Label147
BaseField.Text: Please read the LTA Instructions prior to answering this question.

BaseField.Name: Label257
BaseField.Text: If you made any contacts, please provide information in Question 12

BaseField.Name: Historic Sites Native American Cultural Resources Review
BaseField.AccessibilityLabel: Historic Sites Native American Cultural Resources Review

BaseField.Name: Label149
BaseField.Text: 12. Additional information (attach additional pages if necessary):

BaseField.Name: Label258
BaseField.Text: If applicable, include mitigation measurers from a geotechnical memo, letter, or report.

BaseField.Name: Label261
BaseField.Text: Review FPA/N instructions for required plan information for hydraulic projects in or over Type S, F or complex N Water.

BaseField.Name: Label262
BaseField.Text: Prior to submitting your application, please ensure all required documentation has been uploaded to your LTA Step1.

BaseField.Name: Payment Not Required
BaseField.AccessibilityLabel: My application does not require payment for one of the following reasons
BaseField.Text: My application does not require payment for one of the following reasons:

BaseField.Name: Label284
BaseField.Text: • 45-Day Resubmittal of a Withdrawn or Disapproved LTA Step 1:

BaseField.Name: Label264
BaseField.Text: *The information on this application/notification is true.

BaseField.Name: Label263
BaseField.Text: *We understand this proposed forest practice is subject to:

BaseField.Name: Label154
BaseField.Text: • The Forest Practices Act and Rules, AND

BaseField.Name: Label155
BaseField.Text: • All other federal, state or local regulations.

BaseField.Name: Label267
BaseField.Text: *Compliance with the Forest Practices Act and Rules does not ensure compliance with the Endangered Species Act or other federal, state or local laws.

BaseField.Name: Label266
BaseField.Text: *The following may result in an unauthorized incidental take of certain endangered or threatened fish species:

BaseField.Name: Label159
BaseField.Text: • Conversion of land to non-forestry use.

BaseField.Name: Label160
BaseField.Text: • Harvesting within the maximum RMZ on a 20-acre exempt parcel that was acquired after June 5, 2006.

BaseField.Name: Label161
BaseField.Text: • Equipment crossings or fords that are in or over Type S and F Waters.

BaseField.Name: Label163
BaseField.Text: • If you find or suspect you have found an archeological object or Native American cairn, grave, or glyptic record, immediately cease disturbance activity, protect the area and promptly contact the Department of Archaeology and Historic Preservation at 360-586-3077.

BaseField.Name: Label164
BaseField.Text: • If you find or suspect you have found human skeletal remains, immediately cease disturbance activity, protect the area, and contact the County Coroner or Medical Examiner and local law enforcement as soon as possible. Failure to report human remains is a misdemeanor.

BaseField.Name: Label165
BaseField.Text: The landowner understands that by signing and submitting this LTA they are authorizing the Department of Natural Resources to enter the property in order to review the proposal, inspect harvest operations, and monitor compliance for up to three years after its expiration date. RCW 76.09.150.

BaseField.Name: Label181
BaseField.Text: NOTE: Prior to submittal, the proponent must include evidence for when a “Perpetual Timber Rights Owner” is signing on behalf of the landowner. See WAC 222-20-010(4)

BaseField.Name: Label269
BaseField.Text: • If this FPA/N contains a hydraulic project requiring WDFW concurrence review, it will not be available online for public review until after the WDFW concurrence review period.

BaseField.Name: DG Signatures
BaseField.DataFilter: [Contact Information Relation].[Related Record ID] = '{{Form ID}}' AND [Contact Information Relation].[Related Record ID] <> '' AND [Contact Information Relation].[Status] <> 'Disabled'

BaseField.Name: Label274
BaseField.Text: You can manage your submitted application by selecting one of the following actions.

BaseField.Name: Label270
BaseField.Text: • Withdrawal Request: Request to withdraw or close your application.

BaseField.Name: Label271
BaseField.Text: • Notice of Transfer: Transfer the approved application or notification to a new landowner, timber owner or operator. See WAC 222-20-010(6)/(7) for additional information.

BaseField.Name: Label272
BaseField.Text: • LTA Step 2 FPA/N: Apply for Step 2 of a validated LTA Step 1. See WAC 222-20-016(2)(b) for additional information.

BaseField.Name: Label312
BaseField.Text: • Copy Application: This action copies all contents of this application, including any uploaded documents. After copying, you can edit the copy and submit it as a new, unique application.

BaseField.Name: DG Fees
BaseField.DataFilter: [Related Record ID] = '{{Form ID}}' AND [Related Record ID] <> '' AND ([Status] = 'Unpaid' OR [Status] = 'Paid')

BaseField.Name: Label43
BaseField.Text: Instructions: This section is only visible to VaultAccess users. Check Administrator Override to remove read-only configurations and show all fields. Select Admin Save to save any changes without applying business rules. Remember to uncheck override and save before closing.

BaseField.Name: Clean Text For Q12 Additional Information
BaseField.AccessibilityLabel: Clean Text For Q12 Additional Information

BaseField.Name: Clean Text For Q12 Redacted Content
BaseField.AccessibilityLabel: Clean Text For Q12 Redacted Content

Group.GroupName: Show Fees Tab Finance Editor Buttons
Show Fees Tab Pay Now Button
Show Validate And Proceed Button Q12

FormScriptItem.Name: btnRemoveSelectedLandownersBusinesses_onClick
btnRemoveSelectedLegalDescription_onClick
btnRemoveSelectedLandownersSigners_onClick
btnValidateAndProceed_onClick
btnPayNow_onClick
btn_Submit_onClick
btnViewDocumentation_onClick
Last_Name_Contact_Person_onBlur
FormScriptItem.Script: VV.Form.Template.FormValidation("Last Name Contact Person");

FormScriptItem.Name: btn_HelpIdentifierActivityMap_onClick
FormScriptItem.Script:
const modalContent = `

if yes, list each one separately. Show them along with their unique identifier on your activity map.

  • Identifier: Enter a unique identifier for each stream segment, stream associated wetland, lake, or pond.
    • Do not use S, F, or N.
    • Show the identifiers on the Activity Map.
  • DNR Mapped Water Type: Enter the water type (S, F, or N) as shown on DNR’s Forest Practices Activity Map.
    • If DNR’s map does not show streams or water bodies for the segment you are identifying, leave this box blank or write “none”.
    • Type S waters are “Shorelines of the State” and are regulated by the local government and DNR.
  • Field Verified Water Type: Enter the field verified water type (F, Np, or Ns).
    • Enter only one water type per unique identifier.
    • You are required to verify Type N waters or any water bodies that exist in the field but are not on DNR’s map.
    • You do not need to verify Type S Water.
    • See Water Typing Requirements and Instructions for Appendix A. Water Type Classification Worksheet here.
    • Leave this column blank for Type S Water. Type S Water is determined by Department of Ecology (ECY) and cannot be changed by any person or agency other than ECY.
  • Site Class: Enter the site class.
    • Site class maps are available on the Forest Practices website here.
  • Bankfull Width: Enter the width in feet measured at bankfull width.
    • For more information, refer to the Forest Practices Board Manual Section 2- Standard Methods for Identifying Bankfull Channel Features and Channel Migration Zones.
  • Segment Length: Enter the length of the stream segment in feet.
  • Is there a CMZ? Enter ‘Y’ if there is a CMZ present, otherwise enter ‘N’.
    • If there is a CMZ present, include details of the physical and historical evidence used to delineate the CMZ on the ground in Question 12.
    • Complete and submit Appendix E. CMZ Assessment Form.

Show the following on the Activity Map:

  • Field verified water types: verify Type N Waters shown on the Activity Map.
  • Draw all streams that are on the ground but not identified on the Activity Map.
  • Cross out (draw a series of x’s or hatch marks) typed waters that are shown on the map but do not exist on the ground.
  • Or map the features using GIS and the Activity Map Requirements on page 36. If you use a GIS map of your own creation, DNR may ask you to include a Forest Practices Activity Map with the area circled for reference.
`; // Calling the CentralMessages function with the modalObject VV.Form.Global.CentralMessages("Help", { helpTitle: "Identifier Activity Map", helpText: modalContent, });

FormScriptItem.Name: btnActivityMap_onClick
Country_onBlur
Redacted_Content_onBlur
btn_Resubmit_onClick
btn_HelpLandowner_onClick
Historic_Sites_Native_American_Cultural_Resources_Review_onBlur
FormScriptItem.Script: VV.Form.Template.FormValidation("Historic Sites Native American Cultural Resources Review");

FormScriptItem.Name: btnSendForSignatures_onClick
btn_HelpSmallForestLandowner_onClick
btn_AddAppendixD_onClick
FormScriptItem.Script: VV.Form.Template.AddAppendixD();

FormScriptItem.Name: btn_HelpUnstableSlopesLandforms_onClick
FormScriptItem.Script:
const modalContent = `

If yes, complete Appendix D. Slope Stability Informational Form and attach a map of the areas reviewed, and the locations of any unstable slopes and landforms found.

  • If applicable, attach a geotechnical memo, letter, or report prepared and stamped by a qualified expert.
    • A list of qualified experts can be found on the Forest Practices Rules and Board Manual Guidelines page on the DNR website.
  • If forest practices are occurring on a rule-identified landform, attach a geotechnical report and SEPA Environmental Checklist.

Refer to Forest Practices Board Manual Section 16- Guidelines for Evaluating Potentially Unstable Slopes and Landforms for information on assessing and identifying areas for slope instability.

See FPA/N instructions for additional information.

`; // Calling the CentralMessages function with the modalObject VV.Form.Global.CentralMessages("Help", { helpTitle: "Are there potential unstable slopes or landforms in or around the area?", helpText: modalContent, });

btnAssessFee_onClick
FormScriptItem.Script: VV.Form.Global.AssessFee();

btnCopyApplication_onClick

FormScriptItem.Script: VV.Form.Template.FormValidation("Park Name");

FormScriptItem.Name: btn_HelpWithin50milesSaltWater_onClick
FormScriptItem.Script:
const modalContent = `

Answer ‘yes’ and complete Appendix J. Marbled Murrelet Form if you are:

  • Harvesting timber (including salvage) or constructing roads within 50 miles of saltwater; AND
  • You own 500 acres or more forestland in Washington and you do not have an approved state and/or federal conservation agreement with prescriptions that include the marbled murrelet.

If you have an agreement, submit a copy of the prescriptions (or reference the prescriptions on file at the DNR Region office) and list the forest practices rules that are being substituted.

`; // Calling the CentralMessages function with the modalObject VV.Form.Global.CentralMessages("Help", { helpTitle: "Within 50 miles of Saltwater", helpText: modalContent, });

FormScriptItem.Name: btn_HelpAdjacentMigration_onClick
Unstable_Slopes_Landforms_onChange
FormScriptItem.Script: VV.Form.Template.FormValidation("Unstable Slopes Landforms");

FormScriptItem.Script: VV.Form.Template.AddLegalDescription();

FormScriptItem.Script: VV.Form.Template.FormValidation("Landowner Business Or Individual");

Phone_onBlur

FormScriptItem.Name: Within_500_Feet_Public_Park_onChange
FormScriptItem.Script: VV.Form.Template.FormValidation("Within 500 Feet Public Park");

FormScriptItem.Name: btn_HelpSensitiveSiteInventory_onClick
FormScriptItem.Script:
const modalContent = `

List each one separately and show it, along with their identifier on your activity map.

  • Site Identifier:
    • Enter one unique identifier per site.
    • Use letters and/or numbers.
  • Type of Site: See “sensitive sites” in general definitions, WAC 222-16-010. Enter one of the following:
    • Headwall seep
    • Side-slope seeps
    • Type Np Water intersection or confluence
    • Headwater spring
    • Alluvial fans
  • Describe how you marked sensitive sites on the ground: List the colors and types of markers used to mark the features in the field. Mark the extents of each sensitive site in the field. Markings must be able to last fifteen years. You may list the coordinates and datum type in this space, but they are not required. If you choose to use GPS coordinates, you still need to mark the sites on the ground.
  • Show the following on your Activity Map: Locations, along with their identifiers, of each sensitive site listed on the inventory
`; // Calling the CentralMessages function with the modalObject VV.Form.Global.CentralMessages("Help", { helpTitle: "Sensitive Site Inventory", helpText: modalContent, });

FormScriptItem.Name: Historic_Sites_Native_American_Cultural_Resources_Review_onChange
FormScriptItem.Script: VV.Form.Template.FormValidation("Historic Sites Native American Cultural Resources Review");

FormScriptItem.Name: Within_50_Miles_Saltwater_Own_More_Than_500_Acres_onChange
FormScriptItem.Script: VV.Form.Template.FormValidation("Within 50 Miles Saltwater Own More Than 500 Acres");

FormScriptItem.Name: Email_Contact_Person_onBlur
FormScriptItem.Script: VV.Form.Template.FormValidation("Email Contact Person");

FormScriptItem.Name: btn_HelpRoadAssessment_onClick
FormScriptItem.Script:
const modalContent = `

Show roads, water crossings, spoil areas, and rock pits, along with their unique identifier on your Activity Map.

  • Road Identifier: Enter a road identifier for each road or location of a specific road section or water crossing being assessed. Include all existing forest roads within the area covered by this forest practices.
    • Exemptions from this section
      • Public roads such as state and county highways and city streets.
  • Date assessed: Enter the month and year the road or road segment was assessed.
  • Road issue assessed: Enter one of the categories listed below.
    • Water from the road or ditch runs directly into typed water.
    • Water flows under, over, or around the culvert.
    • The culvert keeps filling with dirt.
    • The road has large cracks.
    • The road has sinkholes. (Not a pothole, but a hole that you can’t drive over.)
    • Dirt from the uphill side of the road keeps falling into the ditch-line before regularly scheduled maintenance.
    • Dirt for the cut-slope keeps falling downhill into or near a stream, pond, or wetland.
    • The road crosses typed water (a culvert, bridge, or ford exists).
    • Other (explain).
    • No issues.
  • Comments: Give details that will clarify the location and/or condition of the road or water crossing structure that you are assessing.
    • For assistance in accessing and correcting potential fish passage barriers go to the Small Forest Landowner Office website at: https://www.dnr.wa.gov/fffpp
`; // Calling the CentralMessages function with the modalObject VV.Form.Global.CentralMessages("Help", { helpTitle: "Road Assessment", helpText: modalContent, });

FormScriptItem.Name: Region_onChange
btn_HelpAdditionalInformation_onClick
btn_RemoveRoads_onClick
btn_HelpLandownerBusinessSearch_onClick
Status_onChange
FPAN_Number_onBlur
FormScriptItem.Script: VV.Form.Template.UpdateDocumentPath();

FormScriptItem.Name: btn_HelpWildlife_onClick
btn_HelpLandownerBusinessSignerSearch_onClick
btn_HelpWetlandsType_onClick
FormScriptItem.Script:
const modalContent = `

Wetlands associated with Type S or F Water should be listed in Question 6. Show the boundaries of each wetland, along with its identifier, and Wetland Management Zone (WMZ) on the Activity Map. See instructions for details.

  • Wetland Identifier: Enter a unique identifier for each stream segment, stream associated wetland, lake, or pond.
    • Show the identifiers on the Activity Map.
    • Do not use S, F, N, A or B.
  • Wetland Type: Enter the type of each separate wetland: A, B or Forested.
    • Include all types and sizes of wetlands that are proposed to be filled or drained.
    • For timber harvest in forested wetlands, only include those that are greater than 3 acres in size.
    • To determine wetland type, refer to WAC 222-16-035.
  • Total Wetland Area: Enter the total area (in acres) of each distinct wetland.
    • Do not include forested wetlands less than 3 acres in size.
  • Show the following on the Activity Map:
    • Boundaries, types (A, B, and/or forested wetlands greater than 3 acres), and identifiers of all wetlands within 200 feet of the proposed.
    • Wetland management zones (WMZs).
`; // Calling the CentralMessages function with the modalObject VV.Form.Global.CentralMessages("Help", { helpTitle: "Wetlands Type", helpText: modalContent, });

FormScriptItem.Name: btnHelpAssessFee_onClick
btn_AddAppendixJ_onClick
FormScriptItem.Script: VV.Form.Template.AddAppendixJ();
